prompts.prompt_lead: Find the transcript for the previous Claude Code session in this repo. Start from the
prompts.expected_response_shape: Claude locates the most recent non-current .jsonl, reads key portions, produces a 4-point structured summary, then diffs against git log/diff output and reports agreements and gaps — with literal counts and quoted correction messages.

risks_fired.evidence: Multi-step autonomous research prompt still invites narration before tool calls. Mitigated: body callout immediately before the fence names the failure mode ('Expect a narration before the findings') and gives a recovery ('skim past the opening to the numbered list'). Body-callout carve-out applies — downgraded from med.

Prompt asks Claude to characterise where a previous Claude session drifted and corrected itself — RLHF charity may soften severity. Mitigated: trailing in-fence line ('Report literal counts and quoted text: actual restart numbers, exact correction messages. No softened summary.') is an explicit over-flag instruction. Downgraded from med.

Asking Claude to critique another Claude session's quality (drift, stalls, corrections) invites the sibling-charity variant of self-audit-charity. The same in-fence over-flag line ('No softened summary') provides partial mitigation; specificity demand (literal counts, quoted messages) reduces charitable summary risk.

failure_modes: Claude opens with brief narration of its lookup plan — body callout gives the student the skim-past recovery, so this is expected and handled.
Claude characterises a session's drift as milder than the transcript shows — the 'literal counts' line substantially reduces but does not eliminate this risk.
recovery_paths: Body callout already in place. No further prompt-level changes needed for preamble-before-action.
In-fence over-flag already in place. Self-audit-charity residual is low; accept.
